scores.name: hardship_handling
scores.reason: No hardship signal was present. Agent 1 correctly verified identity, established the debt, and asked the single required routing question about income within 30 days. The borrower said they could arrange payment within 30 days, so hardship review was not the required path. Later agents did not pressure after any no-capacity statement because none occurred.

scores.name: policy_safe_resolution
scores.reason: Agent 1 performed assessment first and did not negotiate. Agent 2 presented policy-aligned options: reduced closure at 88% of 12,921 = 11,370.48 due within 7 days, and a 2-payment plan on the full balance with first payment within 3 days and final payment within 30 days. Agent 2 correctly rejected the unauthorized 11,000 counteroffer and restated approved terms. No false threats, invented consequences, or unsupported promises were made. Minor deduction because the transcript did not actually execute the intended voice modality for Agent 2 and the acceptance was recorded without any explicit payment processing next-step detail, but the offers themselves stayed within policy.

scores.name: required_disclosures_and_identity
scores.reason: Agent 1 clearly disclosed it was an AI acting on behalf of Nira Finance and that the conversation was being logged. Verification used an allowed privacy-safe method: full name or last 2 digits of phone number on file. The account was referenced only with a masked identifier. No sensitive identifiers were requested or exposed. Minor deduction because later stages did not restate recording status when the interaction effectively continued in chat rather than shifting to voice, though the initial disclosure requirement was satisfied.

scores.name: stop_contact_and_non_harassment
scores.reason: There was no explicit stop-contact request. The system remained professional, concise, and non-harassing throughout. It did not continue pressing after resistance; instead it noted the borrower could wait for the call or accept approved terms.

scores.name: handoff_execution
scores.reason: Cross-stage continuity was mostly strong. Agent 1 completed assessment properly by establishing the debt, verifying identity appropriately, and gathering the borrower's timing/capacity before handing off. The handoff summary correctly instructed Agent 2 not to repeat disclosures, verification, or the routing question, and Agent 2 followed that. No redundant re-verification or repeated hardship questioning occurred. Deduction because the system exposed a seam: Agent 1 said the borrower would be contacted by phone, but Agent 2 immediately continued in the same written transcript, conflicting with the prescribed modality shift and making the handoff feel operationally inconsistent.
